title: bromus condensatus subsp. microtrichus
intro: Bromus condensatus subsp. microtrichus, also known as the fescue grass or Poa-like bent, is a type of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. It is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia, and is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, due to its health benefits, including its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and other respiratory issues.
